pattern: A reverse in-order walk visits keys from largest to smallest, so a running suffix sum lets each node absorb everything greater than it in one pass.
hints: In a BST, the keys come out sorted under an in-order traversal; reversing that order visits them from biggest to smallest.
If you keep a running total as you visit nodes in decreasing key order, that total is exactly the sum of all keys already greater than the current one.
Traverse right, then node, then left; add the node's value to an accumulator and overwrite the node with that accumulator.

edge_inputs.description: two nodes, larger on the right

stress.description: random BST built by inserting a shuffled permutation, serialized level-order
stress.note: distinct values keep the BST property
